components.role: Emitter follower transistor — provides current gain and low output impedance
Upper base bias resistor — voltage divider to set DC operating point
Lower base bias resistor — voltage divider to set DC operating point
Emitter resistor — sets bias current and determines output impedance
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, sets low-frequency -3dB point
Output coupling capacitor — blocks DC from emitter node

dsp_model.description: Unity gain with first-order highpass from coupling caps and gentle bandwidth limit from BJT fT

behavioral_description: A straightforward BJT emitter follower that provides near-unity voltage gain with current gain, converting a high-impedance signal to low impedance. Slightly less transparent than an op-amp buffer — the BJT introduces a small amount of even-harmonic coloration under signal peaks due to the nonlinear base-emitter junction. Input impedance is moderate (set by the bias divider, typically 50-200kΩ), so it may load passive pickups slightly more than a JFET buffer. Good for driving long cable runs or low-impedance loads downstream.
